title: Current and former employees sue Meta, alleging discrimination in using AI to conduct layoffs
blurb: A coalition of 26 current and former Meta employees sued the company, alleging its AI-driven layoff systems discriminated against workers on protected medical and family leave. The plaintiffs argue that metrics like performance ratings and AI-token consumption structurally penalize anyone on approved absence, and they are seeking an independent audit; Meta says the claims lack merit and that layoff decisions were made by people, not AI.

title: Google DeepMind chief Demis Hassabis calls for U.S. to spearhead AI standards body
blurb: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis called for the U.S. to lead a new AI standards body that would oversee frontier models and assess national security risks like cybersecurity, nuclear, and bio threats. He proposed a federally overseen public-private partnership modeled on FINRA, funded largely by industry, echoing similar recent calls from Anthropic's Dario Amodei and OpenAI's Sam Altman.
